title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During a group outing to Dartmoor, Mrs. Gardner criticizes Mr. Bret for his intrusive behavior and for distributing unrequested photographs of the party. Guided by Hercule Poirot, the group visits a grotto and a fairy hole before having a picnic by a river. During the excursion, Emily Bristow becomes dizzy while crossing a narrow wooden bridge and requires assistance from Poirot and Patrick Redfern.

Upon returning to the hotel near Loch Cardip, the maid, Gloria Naraqot, informs Mrs. Redfern that Linda Marshall will not wake up. Poirot and others find Linda in her room with an unusual complexion and barely audible breathing. Beside her, Poirot discovers a note written in student handwriting in which Linda confesses to killing Elena Marshall and expresses regret.

Dr. Nesden arrives and informs the group, including Mr. Marshall, the Redferns, and Rosamond Darnley, that Linda's chances of survival are slim. The maid testifies that she saw Linda taking a bottle from Mrs. Redfern's bathroom cabinet. Mrs. Redfern admits the bottle contained her potent sleeping pills, which she had previously given to Linda.

When Colonel Weston expresses disbelief regarding the confession note, Poirot challenges Christine Redfern's alibi. Although Christine claimed to have been with Linda until 11:45, Poirot points out that her sense of time was unreliable. Christine admits that during her walk back to the hotel, she was in a trance-like state, preoccupied with thoughts of leaving the area without telling her husband, which caused her to lose track of time.
